items.text: liminal-server crate exists at crates/liminal-server/ with Cargo.toml declaring liminal as a dependency.
Binary entry point at crates/liminal-server/src/main.rs compiles and produces a runnable binary.
ServerError enum defined in crates/liminal-server/src/error.rs implementing std::error::Error and Debug.
ServerConfig type defined in crates/liminal-server/src/config/types.rs with fields for listen address, channel definitions, routing rules, persistence paths, and cluster config.
Configuration loads from a TOML file specified via --config CLI argument.
Configuration loads from environment variables with LIMINAL_ prefix.
Environment variables override file-based configuration when both specify the same field.

Configuration validation rejects invalid configs (missing required fields, invalid listen addresses, unknown channel types, unreachable persistence paths) with specific error messages before server startup.
Server binds to the configured listen address and accepts wire protocol connections.
Each accepted connection spawns a supervised beamr process for protocol handling.
Graceful shutdown stops accepting new connections and drains existing connections within a configurable timeout.
Graceful shutdown flushes all durable channel state to haematite before process exit.
Graceful shutdown notifies connected subscribers that the server is shutting down before closing connections.
Server discovers cluster peers via configured seed node addresses.
Cluster membership uses beamr distribution protocol for node joining, leaving, and failure detection.
Channel subscriptions span cluster nodes: a subscriber on node A receives messages published on node B.
HTTP health endpoint at /health returns 200 when the server process is alive.
HTTP readiness endpoint at /ready returns 200 only when startup is complete: listener bound, config loaded, and cluster membership established (if configured).
